curation_notes: Re-curation. Patch commit modifies a single file (mooncake_pipe.py). The diff replaces pickle-based send_pyobj/recv_pyobj calls with safe struct.pack/unpack serialization over ZeroMQ multipart messages, and changes socket bindings from tcp://*: (all interfaces) to specific host addresses. Locations derived directly from the patch diff: recv_bytes and wait_for_ack perform insecure deserialization via recv_pyobj(), and send_bytes uses send_pyobj(). The advisory explicitly references recv_pyobj() containing implicit pickle.loads(). Checked sibling pynccl_pipe.py in the same directory — it uses NCCL, not ZeroMQ/pickle, so it does not share the same vulnerability pattern. Vuln class insecure-deserialization confirmed: CWE-502 maps correctly and the patch clearly replaces unsafe pickle deserialization with struct-based parsing. L3 hints improved to be more specific than L1/L2, narrowing to the KV cache transfer pipe subsystem (~3-4 source files).
